5. gateway-conversation-store.ts:26-48 implements a non-atomic findFirst then create pattern with no error handling. schema.prisma:965 shows @@unique([contactId, organizationId]) on ConversationThread; since organizationId is hardcoded 'gateway' and contactId is 'visitor-${sessionId}', a true concurrent second call for the same sessionId would throw an uncaught Prisma unique-constraint error, going to DLQ per managed-webhook.ts:181-193.

One minor correction to the finding: the unique guard key is contactId+organizationId (not contactId+organizationId="gateway") — but since organizationId is hardcoded to the string "gateway" in gateway-conversation-store.ts:24, the effective key is (visitor-${sessionId}, "gateway"), which is exactly what the finding describes. The finding's impact framing holds.

Impact medium (not high): the single-process in-memory ContactMutex would not protect across multiple chat service replicas anyway, so wiring it in its current form is an incomplete fix. The idempotencyKey gap is the more durable fix. Impact is real but the mutex solution is partial — correcting impact to medium.

Every cited claim checks out exactly against the live code:

1. packages/core/src/skill-runtime/hooks/circuit-breaker-hook.ts:8-14 — CircuitBreakerHook implements only beforeSkill(). Confirmed.

2. packages/core/src/skill-runtime/hooks/blast-radius-hook.ts:8-14 — BlastRadiusHook implements only beforeSkill(). Confirmed.

3. packages/core/src/skill-runtime/batch-skill-handler.ts:57 — runBeforeSkillHooks is called here. A repo-wide grep of runBeforeSkillHooks returns ONLY this one call site plus the definition in hook-runner.ts. The live conversation path never calls it.

4. packages/core/src/skill-runtime/skill-executor.ts:166-456 — SkillExecutorImpl.execute() calls runBeforeLlmCallHooks, runAfterLlmCallHooks, runBeforeToolCallHooks, runAfterToolCallHooks. It NEVER calls runBeforeSkillHooks, runAfterSkillHooks, or runOnErrorHooks. Zero occurrences of those names in the file.

5. apps/api/src/bootstrap/skill-mode.ts:540-546 — the live hooks array is exactly [GovernanceHook, safetyGateHook, claimClassifierHook, pdpaConsentGateHook, whatsAppWindowGateHook]. No CircuitBreakerHook or BlastRadiusHook present.

6. packages/core/src/platform/modes/skill-mode.ts:73 — SkillMode.execute() calls this.config.executor.execute() directly with no beforeSkill hook invocation.

7. A grep of apps/api/src/ and apps/chat/src/ for "CircuitBreaker" and "BlastRadius" returns zero hits — confirming neither app constructs or wires these classes.

8. The circuitBreakerTrips Prometheus counter (apps/chat/src/bootstrap/metrics.ts:88, apps/api/src/metrics.ts:88) is defined but never incremented in any source file — a dead metric consistent with the finding's note about it belonging to the orchestrator's utils/circuit-breaker.

The finding is real, novel (not in the shipped/in-flight list), and evidence fully holds. Impact/effort medium/M is credible — this is a meaningful safety gap (a misbehaving deployment would not be auto-tripped on the live conversation path) but not a trivial security bypass (governance, safety, consent hooks still fire); the fix is bounded to wiring existing classes.

All cited evidence holds exactly as described. 

`KNOWN_STOP_REASONS` at `packages/core/src/skill-runtime/adapters/anthropic-tool-adapter.ts:73-77` is `Set(["end_turn", "tool_use", "max_tokens"])`. Lines 174-175 throw `LLMAdapterShapeMismatchError` for any stop_reason outside that set. The comment at lines 171-172 names `refusal` and `pause_turn` explicitly as examples of new Anthropic reasons that would hit this throw path — so this is intentional design, not oversight.

The throw propagates unhandled through `skill-executor.ts:222-243` (Promise.race), is caught at `skill-mode.ts:107-110` as a bare `Error`, and returns `failedResult(workUnit, context, "EXECUTION_ERROR", message)` where `message = err.message` = the raw `"[anthropic] unknown stop_reason: refusal"` string placed in `ExecutionResult.error.message` and `ExecutionResult.summary`.

The Anthropic client is instantiated bare at `apps/api/src/bootstrap/skill-mode.ts:341`: `new Anthropic({ apiKey: process.env["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] })` — no SDK-level `maxRetries` or `timeout` options. The executor has its own `Promise.race` timeout guard but that only fires on overlong calls, not on unexpected stop_reason throws.

The test at `packages/core/src/skill-runtime/__tests__/anthropic-tool-adapter.test.ts:289-303` confirms `refusal` throws, validating the behavior is deliberate.

Practical exposure: `refusal` is a documented Anthropic stop_reason for policy-blocked content and could fire for Alex on sensitive medspa topics (e.g., weight-loss drug inquiries hitting a content policy). `pause_turn` is only returned in streaming/extended-thinking contexts not in use here, so it is lower practical risk. The `refusal` path is the real exposure.

Not in the shipped/in-flight context. Novelty holds. Impact medium (refusal rare but possible for medspa vertical) and effort S (add refusal to known reasons + treat as end_turn yielding the model's safe text) are credible.

dimensions.title: Knowledge grounding & answer coverage (fewer needless escalations)
dimensions.laneSummary: Alex's knowledge-grounding layer has a load-bearing plumbing break: the single REQUIRED knowledge source (BUSINESS_FACTS) is read from BusinessConfig.config by the runtime but written by the operator UI into AgentDeployment.inputConfig.businessFacts — two different tables that never meet, and the read table has no seed/producer at all. So Alex runs with empty business facts and, by its own Bucket-B rule, must escalate every hours/pricing/services/parking question — the most common and most conversion-critical lead questions. Compounding this, the entire semantic-retrieval subsystem (uploaded docs, the 'corrections' anti-hallucination loop, conversation summaries) is computed every turn by ContextBuilder but discarded — the alexBuilder consumes only OUTCOME_PATTERNS — making operator uploads/corrections inert while still paying a per-turn embedding API call + pgvector query. The single biggest opportunity: unify the BusinessFacts producer/consumer and seed a real medspa facts blob, which converts a large class of avoidable escalations into instant correct answers.
dimensions.notesOnShippedQuality: Concrete defects found in shipped code: (1) BusinessFacts producer/consumer are mismatched across two tables (apps/dashboard/src/lib/api-client/marketplace.ts:83-90 writes AgentDeployment.inputConfig.businessFacts; packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:97-102 + packages/db/src/stores/prisma-business-facts-store.ts:7-13 read BusinessConfig.config) — operator-entered facts never reach Alex. (2) ContextBuilder.build (packages/core/src/memory/context-builder.ts:140-231) runs an embedding API call + pgvector search + summary query every Alex turn whose retrievedChunks/learnedFacts/recentSummaries are never consumed (alex.ts:112-123 reads only outcomePatternContext) — wasted cost/latency on the hot reply path. (3) renderBusinessFacts (context-resolver.ts:53-59) silently omits the schema field bookingPolicies.advanceBookingDays (marketplace.ts:301). (4) PrismaBusinessFactsStore.get casts the JSON row as BusinessFacts with no validation (prisma-business-facts-store.ts:12) and renderBusinessFacts has no defensive guards, so a malformed config throws a TypeError that fails the whole turn instead of degrading. (5) computeConfidence/the retrieval threshold logic (retrieval.ts:34-47) is exported as computeRetrievalConfidence but has no live caller — dead anti-hallucination machinery. The alex-context-injection-e2e test deliberately filters out business-facts (alex-context-injection-e2e.test.ts:43-45), so none of these are caught by CI.

findings.title: Operator-entered BusinessFacts are written to a table Alex never reads (inputConfig.businessFacts vs BusinessConfig.config)
findings.currentState: The dashboard BusinessFacts editor validates against BusinessFactsSchema then PATCHes the facts into AgentDeployment.inputConfig.businessFacts (apps/dashboard/src/lib/api-client/marketplace.ts:83-90 body `{ inputConfig: { businessFacts: facts } }`; read back from `deployment.inputConfig.businessFacts` at marketplace.ts:75-81). The live Alex builder reads BUSINESS_FACTS from a different store: `stores.businessFactsStore.get(orgId)` (packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:97-102), which is PrismaBusinessFactsStore.get reading `BusinessConfig.config` keyed by organizationId (packages/db/src/stores/prisma-business-facts-store.ts:7-13). No runtime code anywhere reads `inputConfig.businessFacts` (verified by repo grep). The two paths never meet.
findings.opportunity: BUSINESS_FACTS is the ONLY required:true context in Alex's SKILL.md (skills/alex/SKILL.md:69-72) and the sole source the prompt's 'Business Knowledge Rules' permit Alex to answer hours/pricing/services/parking/prep/policy from (SKILL.md:138-154, 289-306). Because the operator's diligently-filled facts land in inputConfig.businessFacts and Alex reads an empty BusinessConfig.config, Alex runs with BUSINESS_FACTS="" and is forced by its own Bucket-B rule to escalate every factual question — the highest-frequency lead question class ("how much is Botox", "what are your hours", "do you have parking"). Every one of those becomes a human handoff instead of an answer.
findings.proposedChange: Unify the producer and consumer on one table. Simplest: change PrismaBusinessFactsStore.get to read from AgentDeployment.inputConfig.businessFacts for the org's Alex deployment (matching the editor), OR change the editor's upsertBusinessFacts to call the org-keyed BusinessConfig upsert (apps/api endpoint backing PrismaBusinessFactsStore.upsert) instead of the deployment PATCH. Add an integration test that writes via the operator route and asserts renderBusinessFacts(facts) is non-empty in the alexBuilder for the same org. Add a provisioning/readiness assertion that BUSINESS_FACTS resolves non-empty before Alex is enabled (mirror assertAlexSkillPackSeeded).
findings.impactRationale: Directly governs booking conversion: with empty facts Alex cannot answer the most common pre-booking questions and escalates them, dropping leads to a slower human queue. Fixing it converts a large class of avoidable escalations into instant correct answers.
findings.risk: Must pick one canonical table and migrate any orgs that already saved via the editor; renderBusinessFacts will throw on a malformed/partial config (no validation in the store), so add a safeParse + try/catch when switching the source.

findings.title: The BusinessConfig table Alex reads has no seed, no API route, and no UI writer — fresh orgs always start with empty business facts
findings.currentState: `businessConfig` is referenced in exactly three source locations repo-wide: the Prisma schema/migration (model BusinessConfig, schema.prisma:563-571, comment `// Full BusinessProfile JSON`), and PrismaBusinessFactsStore (read + an upsert method). PrismaBusinessFactsStore.upsert (prisma-business-facts-store.ts:15-21) has zero production callers (all `.upsert(orgId,…)` hits are marketplace-persona.ts writing the Persona store). No prisma seed (seed.ts, seed-knowledge.ts, demo-knowledge.ts, seed-marketplace.ts) writes BusinessConfig — seed-marketplace.ts writes organizationConfig (a different table) at line 526.
findings.opportunity: Even independent of the inputConfig mismatch (finding 1), the consumer table has no producer, so a newly provisioned medspa org has empty BUSINESS_FACTS. The e2e context-injection test that supposedly proves injection works explicitly FILTERS OUT business-facts (alex-context-injection-e2e.test.ts:43-45 'it requires a BusinessFactsStore, not the…'), so green tests never exercise the populated-facts path — the producer gap is invisible to CI (matches the durable gotcha 'safety/coverage gate needs producer population; hand-built fixtures hide it').
findings.proposedChange: Add a seedDemoBusinessFacts(prisma, orgId) that upserts a valid medspa BusinessFacts (services with prices, openingHours, parking, bookingPolicies, additionalFaqs) into BusinessConfig for demo/dev orgs, wired into the main seed and into org provisioning. Extend the alex-context-injection e2e to wire a real BusinessFactsStore and assert BUSINESS_FACTS renders with the seeded prices/hours. Reuse demo-knowledge.ts's Glow Aesthetics catalog as the seed content so it is medspa-correct.
findings.impactRationale: Without a producer, every new org's Alex is blind to its own hours/prices, escalating routine questions and losing bookings at the most price-sensitive moment of the funnel.
findings.risk: Seed content must be scrubbed/medspa-accurate (fixtures-as-product-copy gotcha); ensure the seed does not overwrite an operator's real facts on re-seed (use create-or-skip, not blind update).

findings.title: Per-turn embedding + pgvector retrieval + interaction-summary queries run on every Alex turn but their results are thrown away
findings.currentState: alexBuilder calls services.contextBuilder.build(...) and consumes ONLY two of its outputs: `OUTCOME_PATTERNS = builtCtx.outcomePatternContext` and `injectedPatternIds` (packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:112-123). ContextBuilder.build (packages/core/src/memory/context-builder.ts:140-231) eagerly runs `knowledgeRetriever.retrieve(query,…)` (Voyage embedding API call + pgvector similarity search), `deploymentMemoryStore.listHighConfidence`, and `interactionSummaryStore.listByDeployment` in a Promise.all, and returns retrievedChunks/learnedFacts/recentSummaries — but no code in skill-runtime renders or reads those three fields (verified grep: only outcomePatternContext/injectedPatternIds are consumed). There is no {{RETRIEVED_KNOWLEDGE}}/{{RECENT_SUMMARIES}}/{{LEARNED_FACTS}} placeholder in SKILL.md and no renderer for them anywhere.
findings.opportunity: This is simultaneously a cost/latency leak and a knowledge-grounding gap. Cost/latency: when VOYAGE_API_KEY is set (apps/api/src/app.ts:259; ClaudeEmbeddingAdapter.available=true), every Alex turn pays a Voyage embedding round-trip + a pgvector query + a summary query purely to fetch OUTCOME_PATTERNS, then discards the retrieval/summary results — added p50 latency on the user-facing reply path and per-message API spend for zero benefit. Grounding: retrievedChunks would carry uploaded operator documents and corrections; recentSummaries would carry prior-conversation memory — both could let Alex answer more without escalating.
findings.proposedChange: Decide one of two intents and implement it. (A) If grounding is wanted: add a {{RETRIEVED_KNOWLEDGE}} (and optionally {{RECENT_SUMMARIES}}) section to SKILL.md, render builtCtx.retrievedChunks/recentSummaries in alexBuilder (with the existing confidence floor — see finding 6), feeding curated docs + corrections + prior-conversation memory into the answer. (B) If grounding is NOT wanted yet: stop computing it — give ContextBuilder a `patternsOnly`/`skipRetrieval` flag (default for Alex) so build() does not call knowledgeRetriever.retrieve or interactionSummaryStore when only OUTCOME_PATTERNS is consumed, eliminating the per-turn embedding call and DB queries.
findings.impactRationale: Cost/latency: removes a wasted embedding API call + 2 DB queries from the hot reply path on every turn (faster replies, lower spend). Reliability/conversion (option A): unlocks operator-uploaded knowledge + corrections + conversation memory so Alex answers more and escalates less.
findings.risk: Option A widens the prompt and must respect the token budget and a similarity floor to avoid injecting low-relevance/wrong chunks (hallucination risk); option B must be guarded so a future skill that DOES want retrieval can re-enable it per-deployment.

findings.title: Operator document uploads and the 'corrections' anti-hallucination mechanism write to a vector store Alex never reads
findings.currentState: POST /api/knowledge/upload and POST /api/knowledge/corrections write KnowledgeChunk rows into the pgvector store (apps/api/src/routes/knowledge.ts:88-143 and :222-281). The corrections endpoint specifically constructs `CORRECTION: When asked something similar to … the correct answer is: …` content with the highest retrieval boost (SOURCE_BOOST.correction=1.3 in packages/core/src/knowledge/retrieval.ts:5-10) so an operator can fix Alex's wrong answers. But these chunks only surface through KnowledgeRetriever.retrieve → ContextBuilder.retrievedChunks, which the Alex path discards (see finding 3). They never reach Alex's prompt.
findings.opportunity: There is a complete operator feedback/grounding loop on paper — upload your treatment menu, correct a bad answer — that has no effect on Alex. Worse, uploads via the fallback path write zero-vector embeddings (knowledge.ts:118 and :261), which can never match a real query, so even if retrieval were wired, fallback-mode uploads/corrections would be unretrievable. This is the operator's primary lever to expand answer coverage and stop repeat wrong-answers/escalations, and it is inert.
findings.proposedChange: Once retrieval is wired into Alex (finding 3 option A), corrections become live automatically; additionally ensure corrections always go through the real embedding path (reject or warn when ingestionPipeline is absent so corrections aren't stored as zero-vectors). Add a small e2e: create a correction for org X, send a matching lead message, assert the corrected answer appears (or at minimum that the correction chunk is in the resolved context).
findings.impactRationale: Compliance + conversion: corrections are the safety valve to stop Alex repeating a wrong medical/price claim without a code change; document upload is how an org gets full answer coverage. Both currently do nothing, so every operator 'fix' silently fails and the same escalation/wrong-answer recurs.
findings.risk: Depends on finding 3 landing first; injecting correction text must be sentence-classified by the existing claim gate so a sloppy operator correction can't smuggle a non-compliant claim.

findings.title: renderBusinessFacts omits bookingPolicies.advanceBookingDays and will crash the turn on a malformed facts blob
findings.currentState: renderBusinessFacts (packages/core/src/skill-runtime/context-resolver.ts:16-74) renders cancellationPolicy/reschedulePolicy/noShowPolicy/prepInstructions from bookingPolicies (lines 53-59) but NOT `advanceBookingDays`, which is part of BusinessFactsSchema (packages/schemas/src/marketplace.ts:301). Separately, the function iterates facts.locations / Object.entries(facts.openingHours) / facts.services with no defensive guards, and PrismaBusinessFactsStore.get casts the row `as unknown as BusinessFacts` with no Zod validation (prisma-business-facts-store.ts:12), and alexBuilder calls renderBusinessFacts without try/catch (alex.ts:97-102).
findings.opportunity: advanceBookingDays answers a common pre-booking question ('how far ahead can I book?'); since it's never rendered, Alex must escalate it even when the operator set it. And because the store does no validation, any wrong-shaped JSON in BusinessConfig.config (e.g. a future website-scan BusinessProfile written there, which lacks openingHours/services/locations) makes renderBusinessFacts throw a TypeError inside the builder, failing the entire Alex turn rather than degrading to empty facts.
findings.proposedChange: Add an advanceBookingDays line to renderBusinessFacts (e.g. `Advance booking: up to N days ahead`). Validate in PrismaBusinessFactsStore.get with BusinessFactsSchema.safeParse and return null on failure (so the builder degrades to empty facts instead of crashing), or wrap the renderBusinessFacts call in alexBuilder in try/catch that logs and falls back to empty BUSINESS_FACTS.
findings.impactRationale: Small conversion win (one more answerable question) plus a live-reliability hardening (a malformed config currently 500s the conversation turn instead of degrading gracefully).
findings.risk: Low. Validation tightening could surface that some existing dev row is malformed — that's a signal, not a regression; the safeParse-to-null keeps the conversation alive.

findings.title: The retrieval confidence gate (threshold 0.7, low-similarity cap 0.4) is exported but never invoked — no anti-hallucination floor on retrieved knowledge
findings.currentState: computeConfidence in packages/core/src/knowledge/retrieval.ts:34-47 implements a retrieval-confidence floor (returns 0 on no match, caps to LOW_SIMILARITY_CAP=0.4 below DEFAULT_RETRIEVAL_THRESHOLD=0.7, then min with the LLM self-report). It is re-exported from core as `computeRetrievalConfidence` (packages/core/src/index.ts:197) but has no caller in any live path (the only computeConfidence consumers — engine/policy-engine.ts:548 — use a DIFFERENT computeConfidence from engine/confidence.ts). KnowledgeRetriever.retrieve itself applies no similarity threshold filtering; it returns all topK boosted results regardless of how low the similarity is (retrieval.ts:77-90).
findings.opportunity: If/when retrieval is wired into Alex (finding 3 option A), there is currently no relevance floor — a query with no good match still returns the top-K nearest (possibly irrelevant) chunks, which the model may treat as authoritative and answer from, producing a confidently-wrong fact in a regulated medical context. The confidence machinery to prevent exactly this already exists but is orphaned.
findings.proposedChange: When wiring retrieval into alexBuilder, drop chunks below DEFAULT_RETRIEVAL_THRESHOLD (or only inject chunks whose computeConfidence/ similarity clears the floor) so a weak-match query injects nothing and Alex falls back to its 'I'm not certain — let me get a team member' bridge rather than answering from a low-similarity chunk. Either use computeConfidence at injection time or filter in KnowledgeRetriever.retrieve by threshold.
findings.impactRationale: Safety/compliance: prevents hallucinated answers from low-relevance retrieval in a medical-claims setting; preserves the deliberate 'escalate over guess' contract while still expanding coverage for high-confidence matches.
findings.risk: Too high a floor reduces coverage (more escalations); tune the threshold against the A0 eval before enabling. Only relevant once finding 3 option A is taken.

verdicts.notes:
All six cited code locations verified exactly as claimed.

Dashboard write path: `apps/dashboard/src/lib/api-client/marketplace.ts:83-90` calls `PATCH /api/marketplace/deployments/${deploymentId}` with body `{ inputConfig: { businessFacts: facts } }`. This lands in `apps/api/src/routes/marketplace.ts:283-317` which calls `store.update(orgId, id, { inputConfig })` on `PrismaDeploymentStore` — writing only to `AgentDeployment.inputConfig`.

Dashboard read-back path: `marketplace.ts:75-81` reads `deployment?.inputConfig as Record<string,unknown>` then returns `config?.businessFacts ?? null` — reading from `AgentDeployment.inputConfig`, not `BusinessConfig`.

Alex runtime path: `packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:97-102` calls `stores.businessFactsStore.get(orgId)`. The store is wired in `apps/api/src/bootstrap/skill-mode.ts:130+621` as `new PrismaBusinessFactsStore(prismaClient)`. That store at `packages/db/src/stores/prisma-business-facts-store.ts:7-13` does `prisma.businessConfig.findUnique({ where: { organizationId } })` — a completely different table.

No bridge exists: `PrismaBusinessFactsStore.upsert` has no caller in any production route. The API routes register no endpoint that reads `inputConfig.businessFacts` and syncs it to `BusinessConfig`. The dashboard's Next.js `business-facts/route.ts:39` calls `client.upsertBusinessFacts(id, parsed.data)` which goes to the marketplace PATCH, not to `BusinessConfig`. Grep of all `apps/api/src/routes/` confirms zero references to `businessFacts` outside a test file (`marketplace.test.ts:285`).

Impact confirmed high: any operator using the dashboard BusinessFacts editor gets `BUSINESS_FACTS = ""` silently in every Alex turn. Effort M is credible — fix requires either: (a) changing the PATCH handler to also call `businessFactsStore.upsert(orgId, facts)` when `inputConfig.businessFacts` is present, or (b) changing `alexBuilder` to read from `inputConfig` on the `AgentDeployment` record. Not in SHIPPED or IN-FLIGHT context.

Evidence confirmed with one material nuance: the claim of "no UI writer" is slightly imprecise — there IS a dashboard onboarding component (apps/dashboard/src/components/onboarding/business-facts-step.tsx) and a Next.js proxy route (apps/dashboard/src/app/api/dashboard/marketplace/deployments/[id]/business-facts/route.ts with GET+PUT). However, the dashboard's SwitchboardMarketplaceClient.upsertBusinessFacts() (apps/dashboard/src/lib/api-client/marketplace.ts:83-91) does NOT call that PUT route — it calls PATCH /api/marketplace/deployments/:id which writes to AgentDeployment.inputConfig.businessFacts (a different table). The actual BusinessConfig table and PrismaBusinessFactsStore.upsert() (packages/db/src/stores/prisma-business-facts-store.ts:15-21) have zero production callers confirmed. alexBuilder reads BUSINESS_FACTS exclusively via businessFactsStore.get(orgId) → prisma.businessConfig.findUnique (packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:97-101; apps/api/src/bootstrap/skill-mode.ts:130, 621). No seed file (seed.ts, seed-knowledge.ts, demo-knowledge.ts, seed-marketplace.ts — seed-marketplace.ts:526 writes organizationConfig not BusinessConfig) writes to BusinessConfig. The e2e test at packages/core/src/skill-runtime/__tests__/alex-context-injection-e2e.test.ts:43-45 explicitly filters out business-facts kind with comment "requires a BusinessFactsStore." The runtime disconnect is real: operator-entered facts via onboarding land in AgentDeployment.inputConfig.businessFacts while Alex reads from BusinessConfig, so BUSINESS_FACTS is always empty string for every live org. Impact high is credible — every Alex conversation is missing structured service/pricing/hours context. Effort S is accurate (seed upsert + wire the existing upsert into provisioning path).

Evidence holds exactly as cited. In packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:121-122, alexBuilder reads only builtCtx.outcomePatternContext and builtCtx.injectedPatternIds. The fields retrievedChunks, learnedFacts, and recentSummaries are never accessed anywhere outside tests. In packages/core/src/memory/context-builder.ts:140-162, Promise.all fires knowledgeRetriever.retrieve (Voyage embedding + pgvector) + deploymentMemoryStore.listHighConfidence + interactionSummaryStore.listByDeployment on every build() call, then packages the results into retrievedChunks/learnedFacts/recentSummaries (lines 164-201) and returns them. skills/alex/SKILL.md has only {{OUTCOME_PATTERNS}} (line 287); no RETRIEVED_KNOWLEDGE, RECENT_SUMMARIES, or LEARNED_FACTS placeholder exists. One nuance: packages/core/src/knowledge/retrieval.ts:61-63 has an `if (!this.embedding.available) return []` guard, so if VOYAGE_API_KEY is absent the embedding call is skipped. However, deploymentMemoryStore.listHighConfidence (used for both pattern and non-pattern facts) and interactionSummaryStore.listByDeployment are real DB queries that run unconditionally when contextBuilder is wired. The listHighConfidence result is partially used (category==="pattern" items flow to outcomePatternContext), but non-pattern learnedFacts and all interactionSummary data are pure waste. Impact is real but slightly overstated at "high" — the Voyage call is gated by key presence, and listHighConfidence is already needed for the pattern path; only the non-pattern category items and summaries are pure dead work. Effort M is credible for either option. Not in SHIPPED or IN-FLIGHT context.

All four cited locations are accurate and the core claim holds:

1. knowledge.ts:222-281 — corrections endpoint always inserts with zeroVector (line 261: `[${new Array(1024).fill(0).join(",")}]`). There is NO `app.ingestionPipeline` branch in the corrections handler, only in the upload handler (lines 88-104). This is actually worse than implied — corrections are zero-vectored unconditionally.

2. knowledge.ts:88-143 — upload handler correctly described; uses `ingestionPipeline` when available, zero-vector fallback otherwise.

3. retrieval.ts:5-10 — SOURCE_BOOST exactly matches: `correction: 1.3` (highest), `wizard: 1.15`, `learned: 1.1`, `document: 1.0`.

4. alex.ts:112-123 — `alexBuilder` calls `services.contextBuilder.build(...)` and receives a `BuiltContext` that includes `retrievedChunks` (defined in memory/context-builder.ts:32, populated at :164-174, returned at :224). However, the builder only destructures `builtCtx.outcomePatternContext` (line 121) and `builtCtx.injectedPatternIds` (line 122). `retrievedChunks` is silently discarded.

5. SKILL.md confirms no `{{RETRIEVED_CHUNKS}}` or equivalent token exists in Alex's prompt template. The only knowledge surfaces are `{{BUSINESS_FACTS}}` (from businessFactsStore, not pgvector) and `{{OUTCOME_PATTERNS}}` (deployment memory patterns).

6. No reference to `retrievedChunks` exists in any non-test production file outside of memory/context-builder.ts itself (grep confirmed).

Finding is novel — not in SHIPPED context or any in-flight PR (#791, #794, #782). Impact medium / effort M is credible: the fix requires adding a template token to SKILL.md and wiring `builtCtx.retrievedChunks` in alexBuilder parameters, but is gated on "finding 3" (retrieval wiring). The corrections zero-vector bug is an additional sharpening the finding partially surfaces but understates.

All four cited locations check out. (1) advanceBookingDays omission: context-resolver.ts:53-59 renders cancellationPolicy/reschedulePolicy/noShowPolicy/prepInstructions from bookingPolicies but has no line for advanceBookingDays, which is present in BusinessFactsSchema at marketplace.ts:301 as an optional numeric field. Confirmed gap. (2) Unvalidated cast: prisma-business-facts-store.ts:12 does exactly `row.config as unknown as BusinessFacts` with no safeParse or error handling. Confirmed. (3) No try/catch in alexBuilder: alex.ts:96-102 calls renderBusinessFacts(facts) bare inside an `if (facts)` block with no exception boundary. Confirmed. The "unguarded iteration" sub-claim is somewhat overstated — locations/openingHours/services are required (non-optional) in BusinessFactsSchema (marketplace.ts:277,287,295), so they can only be undefined if the cast produces malformed data, which requires corrupted DB state. The crash risk is real but theoretical. The advanceBookingDays omission is the genuine and actionable finding: Alex cannot answer "how far in advance can I book?" from stored facts. Not in shipped or in-flight context. Impact is slightly overstated as medium — it is a low-frequency gap on an optional field, but the fix (one line) is trivially small. Effort S is correct.

All cited evidence verified against main:

1. `packages/core/src/knowledge/retrieval.ts:34-47` — `computeConfidence` exists exactly as described: returns 0 on no match, caps to `LOW_SIMILARITY_CAP=0.4` below `DEFAULT_RETRIEVAL_THRESHOLD=0.7`, takes `min` with `llmSelfReport`.

2. `packages/core/src/index.ts:197` — re-exported as `computeRetrievalConfidence`. Confirmed.

3. `packages/core/src/engine/policy-engine.ts:548` — calls a completely DIFFERENT `computeConfidence` imported from `./confidence.js` (engine/confidence.ts), which takes `{riskScore, schemaComplete, hasRequiredParams, retrievalQuality, toolSuccessRate}` — entirely distinct from the retrieval one. Name collision, no shared logic.

4. `packages/core/src/knowledge/retrieval.ts:77-90` — `KnowledgeRetriever.retrieve` applies source boost and sorts results, but applies NO similarity threshold filter. All topK results are returned regardless of similarity score.

5. Additional finding (not in cited evidence but load-bearing): `packages/core/src/skill-runtime/builders/alex.ts:113-122` — `alexBuilder` calls `services.contextBuilder.build()` but only consumes `builtCtx.outcomePatternContext` and `builtCtx.injectedPatternIds`. `builtCtx.retrievedChunks` is silently discarded.

6. Alex's `skills/alex/SKILL.md` has no template slot for retrieved chunks (`{{RETRIEVED_CHUNKS}}` or equivalent). The only injected context slots are `{{BUSINESS_FACTS}}`, `{{OUTCOME_PATTERNS}}`, and several `PLAYBOOK_CONTEXT`/`POLICY_CONTEXT`/`CLAIM_BOUNDARIES`/`QUALIFICATION_CONTEXT` slots resolved via `ContextResolverImpl` (from static `KnowledgeEntry` rows — no similarity threshold involved there either).

7. `computeRetrievalConfidence` has no callers outside its own definition and unit tests.

Net: the retrieval pipeline runs (embeds query, searches, boosts, sorts), returns chunks to `ContextBuilder.build()`, which places them in `retrievedChunks` in the returned struct — and then `alexBuilder` throws that struct away without using `retrievedChunks`. The confidence gate function is unused in any live path. The finding accurately describes a dead code / missing wiring situation.

Impact/effort assessment: medium/S is credible. The fix is straightforward (add a threshold filter in `KnowledgeRetriever.retrieve` or in the `ContextBuilder` loop, and expose `retrievedChunks` via a template slot in SKILL.md + add the parameter in `alexBuilder`). Impact is genuinely medium — Alex currently has no RAG anti-hallucination floor, but this lane only matters when `knowledgeStore` is populated with per-org documents; an empty store means no risk today.

topThemes: PRODUCER/CONSUMER WIRING GAPS DOMINATE — the machinery is built but the two ends never meet. This is the single most recurrent failure shape across lanes: BusinessFacts written to inputConfig.businessFacts but read from BusinessConfig.config (knowledge); booking.create never advances opportunity stage and both registerBookingCreateHook callers are no-ops (booking); trust score never incremented nor read (governance); onboarding writes startingAutonomy but runtime reads trustLevelOverride (governance); ModelRouter/CircuitBreaker/BlastRadius/ContactMutex/TracePersistenceHook all implemented+tested+exported but absent from the live hook array (model, runtime, governance, learning); pilotMode escape hatch wired but set by zero deployments (learning); injectedPatternIds written at finalize but read by nothing (learning). Almost none of Alex's 'advertised' safety/learning/grounding nets actually run on the live path.

THE EVAL & GREEN-CI GIVE FALSE CONFIDENCE PRECISELY AT THE LIVE SEAMS — every lane found a defect that CI structurally cannot see. The eval bypasses resolvePersona (run-conversation.ts:174 casts persona straight to alexBuilder), runs with patterns empty, runs UNGOVERNED with [] hooks, runs router=undefined (Sonnet-everywhere), and the context-injection e2e test deliberately filters OUT business-facts. So the persona-shape crash, the tier downgrade, every deterministic gate/hook, the pattern-injection loop, and the empty-facts escalation are ALL invisible to a green suite. The judge itself is non-deterministic (no temp pin) and context-blind (grades against a one-line synopsis), so the regression gate is intrinsically flaky — which is the real reason baseline-lock is stuck on continue-on-error, more than the credits the backlog blames.

OVER-ESCALATION IS THE DOMINANT CONVERSION LEAK, AND IT COMPOUNDS FROM MULTIPLE INDEPENDENT SOURCES — the system is biased to hand leads to humans at exactly the high-intent / high-frequency moments. Empty BusinessFacts forces escalation on every hours/pricing/services question (the most common inbound); the slots NaN-collapse + missing current-date anchor + 'guided' booking-park + no reschedule tool leak the funnel at the booking close; the pre-input gate over-escalates bare 'anxious'/un-negated condition keywords (the exact 'aesthetic anxiety' lead Alex was built to win); the claim-classifier applies no confidence floor so enabling enforce escalates a large fraction of normal turns; and every handoff that results is BLIND (assembler fed messages:[]) so the human inherits zero context. Narrowing escalation + grounding answers is a larger conversion lever than any single feature.

DEAD/MISLEADING CODE & STALE COMMENTS CREATE A SAFETY ILLUSION — multiple lanes independently flagged committed artifacts that imply capability which does not exist: 'canonical-merge cron (PR-3.2d)' comment with no registered function; TracePersistenceHook stale 'as if registered' comment; conversation-pattern + market reference files committed as 'Phase 1a placeholder' stubs with no loader; idempotent:true flag declared but never read; maxWritesPerExecution/writeApprovalRequired defined but never read; emotional-classifier exported from the barrel with zero live importers; bookingLink resolved into PERSONA_CONFIG but never referenced in the prompt; computeRetrievalConfidence exported with no caller. A maintainer debugging 'why doesn't X work' is actively misled.

COST/LATENCY IS BEING SPENT ON THE HOT REPLY PATH FOR ZERO BENEFIT, AND IS UNMEASURABLE — every Alex turn pays a Voyage embedding round-trip + pgvector query + summary query whose retrievedChunks/recentSummaries the alexBuilder discards (consumes only OUTCOME_PATTERNS); live Alex sends no temperature (samples ~1.0); the per-call timeout is dead and in-flight calls are never aborted (paid-for retries leak under a 10-min SDK default); and TracePersistenceHook isn't wired so there is no per-conversation token/latency/cache telemetry to judge ANY of it. The router flip (a cost play) and the caching depth cannot be validated against real traffic because nothing persists the before/after.
completeness.highestLeverage: Ship the BusinessFacts producer/consumer unification + a seeded real medspa facts blob (knowledge-grounding finding #1/#2), bundled with the booking-funnel's two cheapest leaks: inject a current-date anchor into the prompt and SlotQuerySchema.parse() so bufferMinutes defaults (so slots.query stops collapsing to ~1 slot/day and stops false 'availability trouble' escalations). RATIONALE: this is the rare bundle that is simultaneously highest booking-conversion impact, lowest effort, highest novelty, and most launch-blocking. The factual question class ('how much is Botox', 'what are your hours', 'do you have parking') is the highest-FREQUENCY inbound and is currently escalated 100% because the operator's diligently-filled facts land in a table Alex never reads — so Alex launches functionally mute on its most common turn. The two booking fixes are one-line schema/cast changes that unbreak the funnel at its highest-INTENT moment. All three are pure wiring (no new product surface, no Meta/credit dependency), each is invisible to the green eval (the context-injection test filters facts out; the eval bypasses resolvePersona and runs slots stubbed), and together they convert the single largest class of avoidable escalations into instant correct answers and let a ready-to-book lead actually see real slots. Nothing else on the board moves bookings this much for this little code on a pre-launch timeline.
blindSpots: ONBOARDING-TO-FIRST-LIVE-CONVERSATION & OPERATOR PREVIEW: No lane covered the activation funnel before Alex's first real lead. Confirmed there is NO sandbox/test-drive/dry-run path for an operator to preview or QA Alex against sample leads before going live (grep found none in apps/chat or a real preview surface) — so a clinic discovers the empty-facts mute behavior, the persona-shape crash, or wrong tone only on a real paying lead. The provisioning-readiness guard checks the skill pack is seeded but nothing verifies BusinessFacts are non-empty or that the persona shape is interpolation-safe at activation time. There is no 'Alex is ready' end-to-end smoke that exercises the populated-facts + real-resolver path.

WHATSAPP 24h-WINDOW × FOLLOW-UP × RE-ENGAGEMENT INTERACTION: The 24h window IS implemented (whatsapp.ts:59-76, canSendWhatsAppTemplate gating on messagingOptIn outside the window) and the lanes treated it as out-of-scope, but NO lane examined the interaction between (a) the fail-closed governed follow-up tool, (b) the 24h window, and (c) the re-engagement-offer intentClass path. Open questions none answered: when Alex wants to follow up a 'let me think' lead who has gone silent >24h, is that a template send subject to canSendWhatsAppTemplate, and does the follow-up tool know the window state? Does the lifecycle stalled-sweep that re-engages 'stuck' leads respect the window + opt-in, or can it attempt a free-form send that the WhatsApp API rejects? The whole multi-touch cadence is gated by window mechanics that were never traced end-to-end.

CONVERSATION-LENGTH / CONTEXT-WINDOW ECONOMICS ON LONG CHATS: skill-mode.ts:58 takes rawMessages = conversationParam?.messages ?? [] with NO slicing/windowing visible at the executor seam — the bound (if any) lives upstream in whoever populates conversation.messages, and no lane verified it exists. On a long medspa back-and-forth (qualification + objections + reschedule haggling) the FULL transcript replays every turn, growing token cost linearly and — critically — invalidating prompt caching as the suffix grows, so the caching win silently decays exactly on the longest (most expensive) conversations. No lane measured tokens-as-a-function-of-conversation-depth or whether there is a history-summarization/windowing strategy. This is a distinct cost axis from the per-turn retrieval waste the knowledge lane found.

CHANNEL-SPECIFIC BEHAVIORAL DIVERGENCE (WhatsApp vs dashboard-chat vs other adapters): The audit treated 'Alex' as channel-agnostic, but typing-delay/human-pacing exists per-adapter (whatsapp.ts, telegram.ts) and WhatsApp has hard 1024-char/template/media constraints the dashboard chat does not. No lane checked: does Alex's multi-slot booking offer (3-5 options) fit a single WhatsApp message, or get truncated/split awkwardly? Does the qualification sidecar / intent tag survive WhatsApp's formatting? Is the same SKILL.md prompt optimal for an async WhatsApp bubble cadence vs a synchronous web chat? Channel-specific message-shape and pacing tuning is unexamined.

OBSERVABILITY / ALERTING ON LIVE ALEX (the on-call view): Sentry is bootstrapped (apps/api/src/bootstrap/sentry.ts) but NO lane verified what actually pages a human when Alex degrades. The governance lane noted no telemetry on approval-parks/denies; the model lane noted no token/latency telemetry; but nobody asked: if Alex starts silently denying every lead (the fail-closed-on-identity-miss landmine), or empty-facts-escalating 100% of leads, or leaking raw error strings — is there ANY alert, dashboard, or anomaly signal, or does it surface only as a weeks-later booking-rate dip? There is no defined SLO/alert on conversion-rate, escalation-rate, deny-rate, or first-response latency for the live agent.

A/B EXPERIMENTATION / ONLINE-LEARNING INFRA & COLD-START: Confirmed there is NO experiment/variant/bucket framework in packages/core (grep found only unrelated 'variant'/'bucket' hits). So there is no way to A/B a prompt change, a router-tier rule, an objection-close phrasing, or a temperature setting against live booking rate — every change ships globally and is judged by lagging counts. Combined with the eval-measurement lane's 'zero online quality measurement' finding, Alex cannot learn from production in any controlled way. Cold-start is also unaddressed: the FIRST clinic / first patterns / first leads have empty OUTCOME_PATTERNS and (today) empty facts — there is no curated cold-start prompt or starter pattern set, so Alex is at its weakest exactly when a pilot is forming its first impression.

DEEPER MEDSPA-VERTICAL DOMAIN DEPTH & SG/MY REGULATORY SPECIFICITY: The lanes covered the FOUR archetype playbook stubs being empty, but none assessed whether the actual domain CONTENT is sufficient: SG (HSA / Private Hospitals and Medical Clinics Act, advertising restrictions on medical procedures) and MY (MOH / Medicine Advertisements Board Act) have specific rules on what an aesthetic clinic may claim/advertise — does claim-boundaries.md actually encode these, or just generic 'don't diagnose'? Pricing-quote norms (consult-first vs published price), GST/SST handling, common treatment vocabulary (e.g. Rejuran/profhilo/HIFU brand asks), and deposit/no-show policy norms specific to the region were not audited for correctness depth. The minor-age and contraindication findings touch safety, but the affirmative marketing-compliance content depth is unverified.

COMPETITOR / PARITY & THE 'WHY ALEX vs A HUMAN SDR' BAR: No lane established what 'good' looks like externally — response speed parity (a human clinic replies in minutes during hours; does Alex's pacing/typing-delay + any approval-park make it SLOWER?), feature parity with off-the-shelf medspa chat tools (deposit collection, payment links, intake forms, Instagram/Lemon8 DM ingestion), and the show-rate mechanics (reminders, deposits) that actually move medspa revenue but are 'designed not built'. The audit is inward-facing; it never asked whether Alex clears the bar that makes a clinic fire its human front-desk chat, which is the real adoption threshold.

DATA-RETENTION / CONSENT-LIFECYCLE & PII-AT-REST FOR A MEDICAL CHANNEL: PII minimization at the channel boundary shipped and the stage.update PII re-leak was caught, but no lane examined retention/erasure: how long are lead transcripts, extracted patterns (which may embed health concerns like 'acne scars', 'wants fillers'), and DeploymentMemory rows kept, and is there a deletion/right-to-erasure path? Patterns extracted from booked conversations may encode sensitive health context indefinitely with no decay-to-deletion. For a regulated medical vertical this is a compliance dimension entirely outside the 10 lanes.

[] with no slice/window, confirming the conversation-length/cost/cache-decay blind spot is real and distinct from the per-turn retrieval-waste finding.
